jokes.text: Then get Bob Loblaw to hire to hire me another surrogate. That's kind of the point. Mr. Loblaw no longer works for us, because someone wanted a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Family needs new lawyer Bob Loblaw
jokes.punchline: The lawyer's name sounds like 'blah blah blah' and he quit because Lindsay wanted to date him
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development wordplay with lawyer whose name sounds like nonsense, plus character-specific comedy about Lindsay ruining professional relationships

jokes.text: I'm talking about all of you getting jobs. [beat] Who threw the tomato?
jokes.setup: Michael announces the family needs to get jobs
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately throws a tomato at him off-screen
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing and economy - the family's horror at getting jobs expressed through immediate violence

jokes.text: Oh, no, not the Milford Academy. Children were neither were to be seen nor heard at the school Buster had attended.
jokes.setup: Michael reacts with horror to private school mention
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it's the school where children are neither seen nor heard - Buster's school
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that explains Buster's weird personality while showing Michael's justified concern

jokes.text: Oh, great. That sounds like another one of those gradeless, structureless, new age feel-gooderies.
jokes.setup: Lindsay describes progressive school philosophy
jokes.punchline: Michael dismisses it as 'feel-gooderies' - made-up word combining feel-good with bakeries
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Michael's cynical personality and dismissive attitude toward progressive education

jokes.text: The boy who couldn't cry. He's a robot! Michael can cry. He doesn't want to rust. Yes, he's like the steel man from The Wizard From Oz. Tin man? Is that what he's called? He knows.
jokes.setup: Family discusses Michael's emotional unavailability
jokes.punchline: Escalates from robot to rusting to Wizard of Oz, but they can't even remember 'Tin Man' correctly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and family dynamic comedy, though the Wizard of Oz reference feels slightly forced

jokes.text: You know, Mom, I think the only time you ever cooked for us, was the morning Rosa's mom died. You gave us cereal in an ashtray.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lindsay taking over cooking duties
jokes.punchline: Reveals Lucille's one cooking attempt was serving cereal in an ashtray during family crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that perfectly captures Lucille's terrible parenting with specific, absurd detail. The ashtray detail elevates it from generic bad parenting to memorably awful.

jokes.text: As what, a waiter? Can I get you something, madam? I will have a vodka, though. What? Oh.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Gob could work as a waiter
jokes.punchline: Gob mimes being a waiter, Lucille immediately orders a real drink without realizing it's a bit
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup that establishes Gob's waiter storyline and shows family obliviousness

jokes.text: Instead of us all selling out and becoming housewives and waiters, why don't we have a big event? Some sort of 'save our Bluths' type thing.
jokes.setup: Family discussing desperate measures
jokes.punchline: Gob proposes 'Save Our Bluths' which matches the episode title 'S.O.B.S'
jokes.explanation: Meta-textual humor that connects to episode title, though more clever than funny

jokes.text: We'll throw a legal defense fund dinner. Ask for donations. As long as we don't look desperate.
jokes.setup: Family planning fundraising strategy
jokes.punchline: Michael wants to beg for money while not looking like they're begging
jokes.explanation: Solid character work showing Michael's delusional pride

jokes.text: Please tell your friends about this show.
jokes.setup: Discussion about family appearing desperate
jokes.punchline: Narrator breaks fourth wall to desperately ask viewers to tell friends about the show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show's actual low ratings situation, delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Shows the desperation the characters are discussing.

jokes.text: I found that canned ham that we'd had forever, and I put it in a pot of boiling water, and guess what I'm calling it? Soup? Hot ham water.
jokes.setup: Lindsay proudly describes her cooking achievement
jokes.punchline: She's made 'hot ham water' - literally just ham in water
jokes.explanation: 'Hot ham water' became an iconic Arrested Development quote. Perfect character comedy showing Lindsay's complete incompetence while she's proud of herself.

jokes.text: Yes, he's a regular Freddy Wilson, that one. I don't know that reference. I don't either. I don't know either. It's this guy.
jokes.setup: Tobias makes what sounds like a cultural reference about George Michael
jokes.punchline: Nobody knows the reference, including Tobias himself, who just points at George Michael
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tobias character comedy - he makes up references to sound smart but gets caught when everyone admits ignorance

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm just taking a three real quick, so I can get a cream soda. Oh, I'll get that for you. No, I will get it. I have to get it myself.
jokes.setup: George Michael says he's taking a break to get soda
jokes.punchline: He insists on getting it himself in an obsessive way, revealing OCD behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment showing George Michael's anxiety, though more plot setup than pure comedy

jokes.text: You know, Michael, if I may take off my acting pants for a moment and pull my analrapist stocking over my head, George Michael has been acting strange lately.
jokes.setup: Tobias wants to give professional psychological advice
jokes.punchline: His mixed metaphor creates disturbing imagery of removing 'acting pants' and putting analyst/therapist stockings over his head
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias word-mangling with sexual undertones he's oblivious to. The mixed metaphor creates absurd mental images.

jokes.text: I think he may have developed what we in the soft sciences refer to obsessive-compulsive disorder. Or the O.C. disorder. Don't call it that.
jokes.setup: Tobias diagnoses George Michael with OCD using proper terminology
jokes.punchline: He immediately dumbs it down to 'the O.C. disorder' and Michael shuts it down
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay joke about the TV show 'The O.C.' while also showing Tobias trying to sound professional but failing

jokes.text: It says, 'over.' Probably have to crack this open to be sure, but this sounds like an expulsion letter.
jokes.setup: Family reads what they think is a glowing recommendation letter for Maeby
jokes.punchline: Michael notices it says 'over' and realizes without opening it that it's bad news
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about how obvious bad news can be if you pay attention to details

jokes.text: Actually, Maeby had been on the set of a film called Snowboarding School 2, in her secret life as a film executive.
jokes.setup: Family thinks Maeby was at boarding school
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals she was actually on set of 'Snowboarding School 2' as a film executive
jokes.explanation: Good ironic revelation that connects her absence to ongoing subplot, though not particularly surprising

jokes.text: Non-traditional mother. Yes, you're right. After all, I am her father.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests Tobias could be a non-traditional mother
jokes.punchline: Tobias agrees, missing the point that he's the father, not mother
jokes.explanation: Standard Tobias confusion about gender roles, though not as clever as his usual wordplay

jokes.text: Your drink, madam. Can I get you anything? Get that one out of here. As you please. But Lucille had never made eye contact with a waiter.
jokes.setup: Gob continues his waiter bit at the country club
jokes.punchline: Lucille treats him like an actual waiter because she never looks at service staff
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy that shows both Lucille's classism and sets up Gob's accidental employment. The narrator explanation makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: We're all out of the halibut. The joke was over... but the lunch rush was just beginning.
jokes.setup: Gob realizes his joke has gone too far
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the lunch rush is starting, trapping him in real work
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and narrator commentary. The dramatic irony of Gob being trapped by his own bit is well-executed.

jokes.text: Yeah, the HBO's not going to want us. What do we do now?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Home Builders Organization not supporting them
jokes.punchline: Michael calls them 'the HBO' making it sound like the cable network
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay joke that works because HBO was known for prestige shows, making the rejection seem even more damning

jokes.text: Actually, I may have an in with Andy Richter. Do you think you can get him? I'm kidding. Of course, not him.
jokes.setup: Tobias claims he might be able to get celebrity Andy Richter
jokes.punchline: He immediately reveals he's kidding because Andy Richter is too famous for them
jokes.explanation: Self-aware comedy about their desperation and low celebrity connections, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Remember what happened when we sent Lindsay to that kind of school? All of a sudden I was the bad guy to her. And you don't respect Mom and you don't respect women. Are you married? Damn school.
jokes.setup: George Sr. warns about progressive schools turning kids against parents
jokes.punchline: Lindsay immediately proves his point by lecturing him about respecting women
jokes.explanation: Good immediate payoff showing cause and effect, with Lindsay unknowingly proving George Sr.'s point

jokes.text: George Sr. did more than just say it. He sent the man a basket of poisoned muffins. Do you know the Muffin Man? There's a reward in it if you do.
jokes.setup: George Sr. threatens to poison teacher
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals he actually did it, connecting to 'Muffin Man' nursery rhyme with wanted poster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that transforms innocent nursery rhyme into serial killer reference. The wanted poster visual gag elevates it further.

jokes.text: Then-- would you like some foam in your coffee? It's called a cuppakeno and wait till you see what it costs.
jokes.setup: Gob is working as waiter
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces 'cappuccino' as 'cuppakeno' while trying to upsell
jokes.explanation: Decent malapropism showing Gob's ignorance about basic service industry terms, though not exceptional

jokes.text: You can say what you want about the Milford Academy-- the kids that came out of there, prepared for life. Does anyone know how to eat one of these? It doesn't do anything.
jokes.setup: George Sr. praises Milford Academy for preparing kids for life
jokes.punchline: Cut to Buster unable to figure out how to eat something basic
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and irony - immediate visual contradiction of the claim about being prepared for life

jokes.text: There was Andy the showoff, Donny the sensitive one, Jerrifth the flirt, Rocky the risk-taker and Andy's stunt double and Emmet who asked that we not show his picture.
jokes.setup: Donny explains his quintuplet brothers
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd names ending with one brother who doesn't want to be shown
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with the invented name 'Jerrifth' and the mysterious Emmet, though the Andy Richter parody is a bit inside baseball

jokes.text: A student either learns and gets an 'L,' or they fluctuate in their learning. What do we get for that? An 'F.' Mr. F.
jokes.setup: Progressive school's alternative to grades
jokes.punchline: The failure grade is still 'F' but they call it 'Mr. F'
jokes.explanation: Sets up major callback to Mr. F storyline while mocking progressive education's euphemisms

jokes.text: Okay, first of all, Variety got it wrong, all right? We didn't even want this project. It was a co-finance with Miramax then, poof, Miramax is gone and we're still on the hook, and it turns out Jamie Kennedy can't even snowboard.
jokes.setup: Maeby explains her film industry problems
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire Hollywood insider complaints ending with Jamie Kennedy can't snowboard
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire showing Maeby's surprising competence, though the references may not age well

jokes.text: We are going to stuff each one of these gift bags with a head shot, some glitter, and a decorative hand soap. And don't forget the funny notes. 'I know where you live. Ha-ha.'
jokes.setup: Tobias explains his marketing strategy to casting directors
jokes.punchline: The 'funny' note is essentially a stalking threat with 'Ha-ha' added
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Tobias's complete social obliviousness. The contrast between threatening message and 'Ha-ha' is perfect.

jokes.text: I walked. I tried hitchhiking, but it's hard to do without a thumb.
jokes.setup: Buster explains how he got to the house
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that he lost his thumb, making hitchhiking difficult
jokes.explanation: Perfect casual delivery of major trauma, typical of the show's dark comedy style

jokes.text: It's so watery. And yet there's a smack of ham to it. It's hot ham water.
jokes.setup: Buster tastes Lindsay's cooking
jokes.punchline: His attempt to be polite about the terrible 'hot ham water'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the awfulness of Lindsay's cooking while showing Buster's politeness

jokes.text: It's like I don't do my homework, George Michael does my homework. And that's when he decided it was time to cheat off of her. It also gave him an opportunity to smell her neck.
jokes.setup: Maeby complains about her dad making her do work
jokes.punchline: George Michael decides to cheat off her while the narrator notes his creepy neck-smelling
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about George Michael's inappropriate crush, enhanced by narrator's clinical observation

jokes.text: He pretends he's the great father all of a sudden and acts like he's all worried about me, but it turns out he just does it so I can help him with his joke of a career. No wonder Mom wanted out so bad.
jokes.setup: George Michael is supposed to write something critical about his dad
jokes.punchline: He delivers brutal honesty about Michael's selfishness and mentions his dead mother
jokes.explanation: Devastating character moment where sweet George Michael reveals deep resentment. The dead mother reference makes it especially dark.

jokes.text: If you didn't have adult onset diabetes, I wouldn't mind giving you a little sugar. Oh, Gob... you could charm the black off a telegram boy.
jokes.setup: Gob flirts with elderly customer about giving her sugar
jokes.punchline: She responds with outdated phrase about charming 'black off a telegram boy'
jokes.explanation: Decent period-appropriate racist language that shows the customer's age while Gob learns flirting works

jokes.text: Okay, we'll just tell you now. She's the one who dies.
jokes.setup: Customer makes racist comment
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately spoils that this character dies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary and dark humor. The narrator's deadpan spoiler delivery is perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: And maybe later you can save a little piece of tail for me. Come on! I hope that isn't the sort of humor Andy McMahon will be doing.
jokes.setup: Gob makes sexual innuendo about lobster tail to his mother
jokes.punchline: Lucille is disgusted and calls Andy Richter 'Andy McMahon'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Gob's inappropriate comment to his mother, plus Lucille's continued name confusion

jokes.text: How dare that Donny Richter. You know, you got to take care of that guy or I will. I'm just going to pull him out of that school.
jokes.setup: George Sr. is angry about the teacher
jokes.punchline: He threatens violence against the teacher then suggests reasonable solution
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s criminal instincts versus Michael's reasonable approach

jokes.text: Well, am I the only one that thinks that this family is finally starting to become sympathetic and relatable? I mean, that's what people want to see.
jokes.setup: Family discussing their various jobs and responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks they're becoming more relatable, immediately contradicted by dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on what audiences want from TV families, immediately undercut by their behavior

jokes.text: She glued my thumb back on. Sister's my new mother, Mother. And is it just me, or is she looking hotter, too? Well, why don't you marry her? Maybe I will!
jokes.setup: Buster defends Lindsay taking care of him
jokes.punchline: Escalates to him finding his sister attractive and considering marrying her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation into incestuous territory that's both shocking and character-appropriate for this dysfunctional family

jokes.text: Yes, he is available. Uh, let me put my, uh, our assistant on the phone with you. Thanks, Deb. Yeah. So he's Tortured Victim #4, right? Yeah, he's really into discipline.
jokes.setup: Tobias gets a casting call
jokes.punchline: Maeby poses as his assistant and the role is 'Tortured Victim #4'
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing Tobias's desperation and the terrible roles he gets, with Maeby's professional competence

jokes.text: Thanks, Deb. Yeah. So he's Tortured Victim #4, right? No, that's great. Yeah, he's really into discipline.
jokes.setup: Maeby taking a casting call for Tobias
jokes.punchline: His role is 'Tortured Victim #4' and she notes he's 'into discipline'
jokes.explanation: Great callback that connects Tobias's creepy parenting to his acting roles

jokes.text: Because I've never done that before, Dad, I swear. I just couldn't think of anything to write about you, so I asked her to write it about her dad. Yeah, it was stupid of me. Now Donny's met you. I mean, he's never going to believe that you were ever up for a part in Wicked.
jokes.setup: George Michael confesses to cheating on his assignment
jokes.punchline: Reveals Maeby wrote about Tobias being up for Wicked, which is absurd
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that explains the mix-up while highlighting Tobias's delusional career aspirations

jokes.text: Oh, no, I'm only making it look like that. He's totally in charge of the situation.
jokes.setup: Appears to be attacking Michael
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's Andy's stunt double just doing fake violence
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy subversion using established character trait

jokes.text: I always pictured a giant six-foot muffin trying to eat me while I was eating him. It's kind of a fantasy I go to a lot now.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the Muffin Man serial killer
jokes.punchline: Character has recurring sexual fantasy about mutual consumption with giant muffin
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully bizarre and disturbing imagery that's both absurd and unsettling in typical Arrested Development fashion

jokes.text: Now that's a clear cut situation with a promise of comedy. Tell your friends.
jokes.setup: Family faces disaster with no caterer and no server
jokes.punchline: Narrator again breaks fourth wall to ask viewers to tell friends about show
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier meta-moment, now with the added joke of promising comedy from their disaster

jokes.text: Poached salmonella? It'll be fine. I'll just put the sauce on them. What kind of sauce is that? Wait, this is the water I thawed the chicken in. Oh. Well, that should go with chicken.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's terrible cooking is revealed
jokes.punchline: The 'sauce' is actually the contaminated water she thawed chicken in
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Lindsay's cooking incompetence with genuinely disgusting revelation. Her casual acceptance makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're the Muffin Man? You poisoned 25 teachers during the '70s? Just the first two. The others were copycats.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes his father is the Muffin Man serial killer
jokes.punchline: George Sr. casually corrects that he only killed two, others were imitators
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to Muffin Man setup with perfect dark comedy. George Sr.'s casual correction about his kill count is hilariously matter-of-fact.

jokes.text: But that's not the truth. We've been given plenty of chances. And maybe the Bluths just aren't worth saving, maybe we're not that likable, you know. We're very self-centered. And my father may be the worst of us.
jokes.setup: George Michael starts to give standard defense speech
jokes.punchline: Instead delivers brutal honesty about family being terrible people
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expected speech with George Michael delivering meta-commentary on the family and show itself. Brutally honest and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Wait, no, no! Where's everyone going? No one's leaving until I get my money! I'll follow you to your cars! Oh, and then that old racist woman choked on Buster's thumb. My thumb!
jokes.setup: Disaster dinner party with guests fleeing
jokes.punchline: Gob threatens to follow them, then casual mention of choking death on Buster's thumb
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with multiple callbacks converging - the racist woman's predicted death happens via Buster's detached thumb

jokes.text: OCD? No, I'm just cleaning up after Aunt Lindsay. She never turns things off. Well... Guess that explains why I saw the hedge trimmer zipping around the drive way.
jokes.setup: George Michael explains his behavior isn't OCD
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes Lindsay left hedge trimmer running and moving around driveway
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag and resolution to George Michael's behavior mystery. The image of an autonomous hedge trimmer is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I love my cousin. Love you, too, pal.
jokes.setup: Michael asks George Michael to tell him what's on his mind
jokes.punchline: George Michael confesses love for cousin, Michael mishears as filial love
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of George Michael's romantic subplot with maximum cringe. Michael's oblivious response makes it even more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: It didn't sound desperate. Well, I guess it really doesn't pay to be desperate. It sure doesn't. And... cut!
jokes.setup: Good news about donations because speech didn't sound desperate
jokes.punchline: Ironic since they are desperate, then 'cut!' reveals this was all being filmed
jokes.explanation: Good meta-ending revealing the documentary nature of the show while commenting on irony of their success
